page: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a bare metal server in the Bare Metal SSD family. It features an AMD EPYC 7443P processor built on the x86_64 architecture, providing 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 2.85 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding approximately 5.33 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 960 GB SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests placing in the top 10% at 3249.57. Multi-core capabilities are strong, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 77709.84 and PassMark CPU score reaching 60061.01, both ranking in the top 25%. Memory performance is highly efficient, as evidenced by a top-tier PassMark memory score of 3105.89 and a strong cached read-only memory bandwidth of 3479.78 GB/sec. Large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidths are average, measuring 134.73 GB/sec and 68.65 GB/sec respectively. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform well, with PassMark CPU encryption scoring 61072.46 and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching 261.89 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, this bare metal configuration offers high cost efficiency by providing dedicated, non-virtualized hardware resources without hypervisor overhead. The high physical core count and substantial memory capacity make it well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and intensive parallel processing. PassMark database operations benchmarked at 22469.21, indicating strong suitability for relational databases and data indexing. Other appropriate workloads include static web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring dedicated CPU and memory allocation.
description: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a dedicated bare metal server featuring an AMD EPYC 7443P processor with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.85 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and a 960 GB local SSD. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU and memory performance, alongside strong multi-core processing capabilities. The server excels in cryptographic operations, multi-threaded decompression, and database tasks, making it highly suitable for resource-intensive enterprise workloads, database hosting, and parallel processing applications that require dedicated, non-virtualized hardware.
og_description: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring a 48-core AMD EPYC 7443P CPU, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and memory performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a bare metal server with a 48-core AMD EPYC CPU, 256 GB RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated bare metal server featuring forty-eight AMD EPYC cores and high-capacity DDR4 memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7443P processor with 48 dedicated physical cores
256.0 GB of high-speed DDR4 system memory
960 GB of local solid-state drive (SSD) storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Optimized for database operations, cryptography, and multi-threaded tasks
